description_long: This is a medium-sized deciduous tree with a slender, upright trunk and a branching structure that extends outward in an irregular, somewhat asymmetrical pattern. The branches are covered with clusters of broad, flat green leaves, giving the tree a leafy but not overly dense appearance. The trunk is brown and relatively narrow at the base, supporting several main branches that split and spread at various angles. The foliage is distributed unevenly, with some branches extending further out and others remaining closer to the trunk, creating a natural, organic silhouette. The leaves are simple and appear to be arranged alternately along the branches. This tree would be commonly found in urban parks, along sidewalks, or as part of landscaped green spaces.
description: A medium-sized deciduous tree with a slender brown trunk and irregularly spreading branches covered in clusters of broad green leaves. The canopy is leafy but moderately open, giving the tree a natural, organic look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the upright trunk and a balanced spread of leafy branches, with foliage concentrated toward the upper and middle sections.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the irregular extension of branches, with one branch extending further outward and the foliage appearing slightly sparser on the lower right.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the asymmetrical branching, with a prominent branch extending to the left and a moderate density of leaves throughout.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tree's vertical structure and the uneven distribution of foliage, with some branches reaching outward more than others.
